page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with no simultaneous multithreading. The system is equipped with 256 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balanced profile with notable strengths and specific architectural tradeoffs. Multi-core performance is solid, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the average tier. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression results. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient for cached small-block reads at 1894.04 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write operations drop to average levels.

This instance is highly cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from high memory density and fast local NVMe storage without requiring high-frequency single-core execution. The combination of 256 GB RAM and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD makes it well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers like Redis, and distributed data processing. It is less optimal for single-threaded compute-bound tasks or large-scale LLM text generation, where performance benchmarks show lower efficiency.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based server instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, 256 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and database performance, alongside high cached memory read bandwidth, though single-core execution and single-threaded decompression tasks represent a performance tradeoff. This resource configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications requiring fast local storage. It is highly suitable for database operations, caching layers, and memory-heavy data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 32 cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated cores, high-capacity RAM, and integrated NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
256 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
